article_meta.title: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas

reasoning: The source (AP) is largely neutral. The model does not inherit loaded framing from the article, but it adds some subjective/interpretive language of its own (e.g., "threatened deportations," "broader movement," "passionate response"). Thus minimal presence of the targeted failure, though other bias dimensions show mild issues.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses interpretive phrases like "broader movement," "high-profile," and "civil disobedience" beyond bare facts.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Asserts a "broader movement" and "passionate response" without direct support in the article.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Describes the protest as "notable for its scale and visibility" and "high-profile" in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Mildly heightened tone ("threatened deportations," "passionate"); not overly dramatic.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Infers protesters’ emotional intensity ("passionate") and intent; minor.
custom_reasoning.slant: Focus follows the article’s emphasis on protesters; no opposing viewpoint present in the source.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits some neutral details (e.g., references to overpasses/110 freeway), but no major perspective missing.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically tinged terms like "threatened deportations" and "civil disobedience" when neutral alternatives exist.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced mention of lack of violence tempers negative elements.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No privileging of elite vs. grassroots sources; recounts AP observations.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Less neutral than the source due to added evaluative language.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures protesters and police presence per article; no other perspectives were offered in the source.
